title: Story 1.5: Navigation Shell & Design System Tokens
description: Set up GoRouter with placeholder routes for Today Calendar, Patient List, Inventory, Day Closeout, and Settings. Create a LayoutBuilder-based responsive app shell (no horizontal scroll at >= 800px desktop width). Define Material 3 color scheme, typography, and spacing tokens in a central theme file. Enforce that only top-level page widgets consume Riverpod providers. Part of Epic 1 (DocCentral-jkt).
notes:
Implementation complete, tests passing, verified via running app.

Files added:
- lib/app.dart (MaterialApp.router, top-level DocCentralApp ConsumerWidget)
- lib/shared/design_system/{app_colors,app_spacing,app_typography,app_theme}.dart
- lib/shared/design_system/widgets/app_shell.dart (responsive shell, pure presentation, no GoRouter coupling - takes child/currentDestination/onItemChanged)
- lib/shared/data/router/app_routes.dart (AppRoutes enum: path+name per destination)
- lib/shared/data/router/app_destination.dart (AppDestination enum: icon+route+toLocalized(l10n))
- lib/shared/data/router/app_router.dart (riverpod appRouterProvider, StatefulShellRoute.indexedStack, 5 branches)
- lib/features/{appointment,patient,inventory,day_closeout,settings}/presentation/*_page.dart (placeholder pages)
- ARB keys added: navCalendar, navPatients, navInventory, navDayCloseout, navSettings (en/fr/ar)
- main.dart updated to boot DocCentralApp instead of inline MyApp/counter demo

Tests: 44/44 passing (flutter test), 0 analyzer issues (flutter analyze), dart format clean.
New test files: app_shell_test.dart, app_router_test.dart, app_destination_test.dart, app_theme_test.dart; widget_test.dart rewritten (was stale counter-demo test).

Verified via running app (Edge/web target - native Windows build blocked by pre-existing unrelated toolchain gap: missing ATL headers for flutter_secure_storage_windows, see follow-up ticket needed):
- All 5 destinations render, correctly localized (FR default), navigate correctly (URL + rail highlight + page body all update).
- Wide layout (>=800px) shows NavigationRail with no overflow - confirmed both in automated widget test and live app.
- OPEN QUESTION: manual browser-window resize to 500px still showed NavigationRail instead of NavigationBar. The automated widget test (app_shell_test.dart) exercises this exact scenario via tester.view.physicalSize and PASSES, so this is likely a resize-event/timing artifact of programmatic native window resize on the web target rather than a real LayoutBuilder bug - but flagging since unconfirmed live. User has taken ownership of the design/responsive-layout layer going forward; deferring further investigation of this specific point to them.

title: Story 1.4: Localization Infrastructure (FR/AR/EN + RTL)
description: Configure flutter_localizations with ARB files for French, Arabic (RTL), and English. Create a Riverpod locale provider that persists per User. Implement locale-aware date (DD/MM/YYYY for fr, YYYY-MM-DD for en), time (24h all locales), and currency (TND) formatters. Verify Arabic locale triggers full RTL layout flip. Enforce no-hardcoded-strings via lint/test. Part of Epic 1 (DocCentral-jkt).

title: Story 1.3: Three-Layer RBAC Framework
description:
Implement role-based permission enforcement at router (GoRouter guard), Riverpod provider (data visibility), and domain operation (PermissionDeniedException) layers using a single shared predicate function hasPermission(Role, Permission).

Define Role enum: doctor, assistant, nurse.

Define Permission enum with action-based entries. The 5 Doctor-only permissions are:
1. canManageClinic — edit clinic profile
2. canManageStaff — add/remove assistants and nurses
3. canAssignRoles — assign roles and permissions to users
4. canViewFinances — view full financial data
5. canUnlockVisit — unlock a completed visit

Unit test enumerates these 5 and confirms doctor=true, assistant=false, nurse=false for each. Also confirms all three layers (router guard, Riverpod provider, domain requirePermission) delegate to the same hasPermission predicate. Part of Epic 1 (DocCentral-jkt).

title: Story 1.2: Drift Local Database with Sync Metadata Pattern
description: Initialize Drift with SQLCipher encryption at rest. Define a sync metadata mixin (id UUID PK, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at nullable, sync_status pending/synced/conflict) that all future tables will use. Create a clinics table as proof-of-concept. Write integration test confirming DB initializes and a row round-trips correctly. Part of Epic 1 (DocCentral-jkt).

title: Story 1.1: Flutter Desktop Project Bootstrap
description: Set up the Flutter project for Windows/macOS/Linux desktop with the correct dependency stack. Keep firebase_auth and firebase_core (Firebase Auth is the authentication layer). Add supabase_flutter (sync layer only), flutter_riverpod, riverpod_annotation, go_router. Add dev dependencies: build_runner, riverpod_generator, riverpod_lint. Scaffold the feature-based folder structure: lib/shared/ and lib/features/. Verify the app builds and launches on desktop platforms without errors. This is the first story every developer runs. Part of Epic 1 (DocCentral-jkt).

title: Epic 1: App Foundation & Infrastructure
description: Establishes the technical foundation for all DocCentral MVP epics. This is PRD section 10 infrastructure work: Flutter desktop scaffold, Drift + SQLCipher local database with sync metadata pattern, Supabase client, Riverpod + codegen, GoRouter navigation, l10n scaffold (FR/AR/EN/RTL), three-layer RBAC framework, audit attribution schema, and test infrastructure. Every subsequent epic depends on this. Covers FR-38, FR-39, NFR-1 (partial), NFR-5, NFR-7, NFR-8, NFR-9, NFR-10, NFR-11.

title: Story 11.4: Conflict Resolution & User Notification
description: Last-write-wins conflict resolution based on UTC updated_at. Non-financial records: remote wins if newer, local wins if newer, discarded side logged. Invoice and Payment conflicts: surfaced as visible user warning (not silent), last-write-wins applied as default but warning persists until dismissed. No data permanently lost; losing version logged for forensic use. Part of Epic 11 (DocCentral-gys).

title: Story 11.3: Pull Remote Changes from Supabase
description: When online, sync engine pulls remote changes incrementally (since last successful pull timestamp) and applies to local Drift DB. Pull failures mid-way leave local data valid and consistent; pull resumes from last safe checkpoint. After successful pull, updated data visible on affected screens without manual refresh. Part of Epic 11 (DocCentral-gys).

title: Story 11.2: Push Local Changes to Supabase
description: On regaining connectivity, sync engine pushes all pending records (sync_status=pending) to Supabase in batches. After successful push, records transition to synced. Push failures retry with exponential backoff capped at 10 minutes. Push is non-blocking: UI remains fully responsive during sync. Part of Epic 11 (DocCentral-gys).

title: Story 11.1: Full Offline Functionality Verification
description: Verify all DocCentral workflows complete without internet. End-to-end test with network disabled: UJ-2 (receive patient + open Patient File), UJ-3 (wrap up visit + take payment), UJ-4 (handle cancellation), UJ-10 (day closeout) all complete successfully. No screen is gated by connectivity except initial sign-in on fresh device. Records created offline have sync_status=pending. Part of Epic 11 (DocCentral-gys).

title: Story 7.5: View Outstanding Balance & Patients with Balance
description: Patient File shows Outstanding Balance = sum(Invoice.total - sum(Payments)) across non-void Invoices, computed at read time, updates within 100ms of a Payment. Dedicated screen lists patients with Outstanding Balance > 0, sortable by balance descending and by days-since-last-payment. Tapping a row navigates to that patient's Patient File. Part of Epic 7 (DocCentral-0y8).

title: Story 7.4: Void Invoice
description: Dentist voids any non-void Invoice with a required reason: transitions to void, records reason + UTC timestamp, Invoice contributes 0 to Outstanding Balance. Prior Payments preserved and shown as refund owed. Void Invoice remains visible in financial history. Assistant returns PERMISSION_DENIED. Part of Epic 7 (DocCentral-0y8).

title: Story 7.3: Record Payment (Full & Partial)
description: Assistant records a Payment on a non-void Invoice: amount, method (default cash), date (default today), optional notes. Saved with recorded_by_user_id and UTC timestamp. Invoice status derived: partially_paid if sum(Payments) < total, paid if >= total. Payment on draft Invoice auto-finalizes it. Multiple Payments per Invoice supported. Payments are immutable (no delete/edit). Unit test confirms Invoice status is always derived. Part of Epic 7 (DocCentral-0y8).

title: Story 7.1: Review & Adjust Draft Invoice
description: After Visit completion, Assistant opens the linked Invoice in draft status. Each Invoice Item mirrors a Performed Treatment (description, tooth number, quantity, unit price, total_price). Assistant adds discount or surcharge adjustments as explicit Invoice Items with adjustment_type. Invoice total = sum(treatment items) + sum(adjustments). Adjustments rejected once Invoice exits draft. Unit test confirms total is never a directly writable field. Part of Epic 7 (DocCentral-0y8).

title: Story 7.2: Finalize Invoice (No-Payment Path)
description: Assistant finalizes a draft Invoice without payment: transitions draft to unpaid, locks Invoice Items and adjustments, logs actor + UTC timestamp. Dentist can unlock parent Visit (if no Payments) to revert Invoice to draft. If Invoice has a Payment, unlock rejected (must void first). Part of Epic 7 (DocCentral-0y8).

title: Story 5.4: Complete Visit & Auto-Generate Invoice
description: Assistant completes an in_progress Visit: status becomes completed, ended_at set, Performed Treatments locked, exactly one Invoice in draft created linked to the Visit with Invoice Items mirroring each Performed Treatment (description, tooth number, quantity, unit price, total_price). Completed Visit rejects all field edits. Unit test confirms one-and-only-one Invoice per completion with Items matching Performed Treatments exactly. Part of Epic 5 (DocCentral-5mr).

title: Story 5.3: Record Clinical Notes & Diagnosis
description: On an in_progress Visit: edit diagnosis and clinical_notes as multi-line text, autosaved on field blur (no explicit save button). On a completed Visit: both fields are read-only. Part of Epic 5 (DocCentral-5mr).

title: Story 5.2: Record Performed Treatments
description: On an in_progress Visit: add Performed Treatments (tooth number, procedure name free text, unit price, quantity), edit or remove them. Each treatment timestamped and attributed to recording user. On a completed Visit: edits and removals rejected. Unit test confirms Performed Treatments cannot be modified on a completed Visit. Part of Epic 5 (DocCentral-5mr).

title: Story 5.1: Start Visit & Mark In-Progress
description: Assistant transitions a checked_in Visit to in_progress. Captures in_progress_at as UTC now(). Controls for adding Performed Treatments, editing diagnosis, and editing clinical notes become enabled. Integration test confirms in_progress_at is non-null after transition. Part of Epic 5 (DocCentral-5mr).

title: Story 4.5: Check In Patient
description: Assistant taps Check In on a scheduled appointment: transitions to checked_in, creates a Visit in checked_in status linked to appointment and patient, sets Visit.started_at = now(). Patient File accessible from same screen showing last 3 visits, Treatment Plan, Outstanding Balance, and Attachments. Part of Epic 4 (DocCentral-rkm).

title: Story 4.4: Cancel & Reschedule Appointment
description: Assistant cancels a scheduled appointment with required reason (patient_cancelled, no_show, clinic_cancelled, rescheduled). Cancellation record created with timestamp, actor, reason. Slot freed in calendar. Rescheduled reason prompts creating replacement appointment linked via rescheduled_to_appointment_id before completing cancellation. No-show pattern flagged on Patient File for repeat offenders. Unit test confirms Cancellation record always has non-null reason/actor/timestamp. Part of Epic 4 (DocCentral-rkm).

title: Story 4.3: Create & Edit Appointment
description: Assistant creates appointment with patient, start time, end time (default 30 min), assigned user (default Dentist), reason. Overlapping appointments for same user require explicit override confirmation. Editing a scheduled appointment logs actor, timestamp, fields changed. Non-scheduled appointments reject edits with APPT_NOT_EDITABLE. Overlap check applies to time/duration edits. Part of Epic 4 (DocCentral-rkm).

title: Story 4.2: Week-View Calendar
description: Week view shows 7 days of appointments, loads within 1 second. Forward/backward navigation shifts by 7 days and loads within 1 second. Appointment statuses (scheduled, checked-in, completed, cancelled) are visually distinct. Part of Epic 4 (DocCentral-rkm).

title: Story 4.1: Day-View Calendar (Today Calendar)
description: Today Calendar renders within 500ms on app launch showing today's appointments in time-grid order. Each row shows patient name, start time, planned-treatment summary, status badge, Lab Work Flag indicator, Outstanding Balance indicator. Status changes update within 200ms. Side panel shows low-stock count badge and appointments awaiting lab delivery today. Part of Epic 4 (DocCentral-rkm).

title: Story 3.4: Edit Patient
description: Assistant edits identity fields (name, DOB, phone, email) and history_notes. Required fields cannot be cleared. Edit log records actor, UTC timestamp, and field names changed. Unit test confirms edit log entry created with correct actor and changed fields. Part of Epic 3 (DocCentral-smy).

title: Story 3.3: Patient File View
description: Opening a Patient File renders within 500ms for up to 50 Visits: identity, history_notes above-the-fold (visible at 1280x800), last 5 Visits, active Treatment Plan, Outstanding Balance (computed at read time, never stored), Attachments list. Sections expand on demand. Outstanding Balance updates within 100ms of a Payment. Part of Epic 3 (DocCentral-smy).
notes:
Implemented as part of the same pass as DocCentral-uw9 (3.1) and DocCentral-2yb (3.2) - see 3.1 notes for full context.

Layout decision (user-confirmed): split-pane (list + detail on one screen, no route change on selection) rather than a separate routed detail page, matching the DentaCore design reference. DocCentral is desktop-only for MVP so no narrow-width fallback was built - always renders the Row split.

_PatientDetailPane renders: identity header (avatar/initials, name, DOB), Overview card (phone, email), history_notes card (above-the-fold per FR-5, only shown when non-empty). Three sections named in FR-7 that depend on epics not yet built render as explicit empty-state placeholders (localized 'not available yet' text), NOT fake data: Recent visits (needs Epic 5), Treatment plan (needs Epic 6), Attachments (needs Epic 9). Outstanding Balance section shows a genuine 0.000 TND (NumberFormat.currency) since no Invoice/Payment rows exist yet (Epic 7) - this is a correct computed zero, not a stub.

Not verified: the 'renders within 500ms for up to 50 Visits' and 'Outstanding Balance updates within 100ms of a Payment' ACs are moot until Epic 5/7 exist - no visits/payments to render or update yet. Revisit those specific performance ACs once those epics land and real data flows into this pane.
close_reason: Patients feature implemented (Drift table, repository, RBAC, search, master-detail UI) and adapted from a user-provided Claude Design mockup to MVP scope; 56/56 tests passing, 0 analyzer issues

title: Story 3.2: Patient Search
description: Assistant searches patients by partial first/last name or last 4 phone digits. Results appear within 300ms on up to 10,000 records. Soft-deleted patients excluded from all searches. Search for 'tra' returns 'Trabelsi' in results. Clearing search restores full active patient list. Part of Epic 3 (DocCentral-smy).
notes:
Implemented as part of the same pass as DocCentral-uw9 (3.1) and DocCentral-3vp (3.3) - see 3.1 notes for full context (design provenance, PatientRecord domain-model workaround for a Drift/riverpod_generator incompatibility, RBAC wiring).

Search: PatientRepositoryImpl.watchAll(role, query) does case-insensitive partial match on firstName/lastName/phone via Drift LIKE query, excludes deletedAt IS NOT NULL rows, empty query returns full active list. UI: debounced-by-nature TextField in the list pane feeding patientSearchQueryProvider -> patientListProvider (Stream<List<PatientRecord>>).

Tests: patient_repository_impl_test.dart covers 'tra' -> 'Trabelsi' partial/case-insensitive match, empty-query full list, soft-delete exclusion. patient_list_page_test.dart covers live search-narrows-list via widget interaction (enterText + pumpAndSettle).

Not separately verified for the literal '10,000 records within 300ms' performance AC - no load/perf test written; flagged as a gap if that guarantee needs explicit proof later (current implementation has no obvious perf red flags: single indexed-by-nothing LIKE query on SQLite, should be fine at that scale but untested).
close_reason: Patients feature implemented (Drift table, repository, RBAC, search, master-detail UI) and adapted from a user-provided Claude Design mockup to MVP scope; 56/56 tests passing, 0 analyzer issues

title: Story 3.1: Create Patient with Paper History Backfill
description: Assistant creates a Patient with required fields: first name, last name, DOB, phone. Email optional. history_notes field accepts pasted paper file contents (no parsing), displayed prominently above-the-fold in Patient File. Required fields validated on save. Unit test confirms Patient cannot be created without all required fields. Part of Epic 3 (DocCentral-smy).
notes:
Implemented, tested, verified via automated widget tests (native app run blocked by pre-existing ATL toolchain gap, see DocCentral-lr4 notes).

New: Patients Drift table (lib/shared/data/database/tables/patients_table.dart), schemaVersion bumped 1->2 with onCreate/onUpgrade migration in app_database.dart. PatientRepository interface + PatientRepositoryImpl (lib/features/patient/{domain,data}/) enforcing RBAC (canCreatePatient) and required-field validation (firstName/lastName/phone) via PatientValidationException. Domain model PatientRecord introduced specifically because Drift's generated Patient row class breaks riverpod_generator codegen in this project's dependency versions (see beads DocCentral-d0b and new memory note) - repository maps Drift rows to PatientRecord before exposing to providers/UI.

Create-patient dialog (_PatientFormDialog) validates all required fields including date of birth; wired via PatientController (AsyncValue.guard).

Design provenance: implemented from a Claude Design mockup (DentaCore prototype) the user shared, adapted down to DocCentral's confirmed MVP scope (dropped odontogram, priced treatment plans, Rx, lab-orders-as-module, multi-provider, insurance, USD - kept avatar/pill visual language, search+filter UX, balance display). Full scope-alignment analysis and adaptation decisions captured in conversation; see Story 3.3 notes for the split-pane layout decision shared across 3.1-3.3.

Tests: patient_repository_impl_test.dart (7 tests: create success, validation failures x2, permission denial, search empty/partial/case-insensitive, soft-delete exclusion), app_database_test.dart extended with a v2 schema test. All passing, 0 analyzer issues, dart format clean.
close_reason: Patients feature implemented (Drift table, repository, RBAC, search, master-detail UI) and adapted from a user-provided Claude Design mockup to MVP scope; 56/56 tests passing, 0 analyzer issues

title: Story 2.4: Language Switch & Localized Formats
description: User switches app language between FR/AR/EN from Settings. Selection takes effect immediately without restart. Arabic switches entire layout to RTL (navigation, form fields, lists, modals). Dates format per locale (FR: DD/MM/YYYY, EN: YYYY-MM-DD), currency as TND, time in 24h. Language preference persists per User across sessions. Part of Epic 2 (DocCentral-59s).

title: Story 2.3: Add Staff User (Assistant / Nurse)
description: Dentist adds a new Assistant or Nurse User from Clinic Settings with name, email, role, and initial password. New User can sign in with assigned Role permissions. Assistant cannot access Add Staff User screen (hidden + router guard blocked). Domain layer returns PERMISSION_DENIED when non-Dentist calls add-user operation. Part of Epic 2 (DocCentral-59s).

title: Story 2.2: Email + Password Sign-In with Persistent Session
description: Users sign in with email and password and land on Today Calendar. Session persists across app restarts via locally cached credentials. Reopening the app reuses the session. Sign-out clears session and returns to sign-in screen. Device offline with cached session: user can sign in and access all offline features. Part of Epic 2 (DocCentral-59s).
notes:
Implemented and tested (8 new tests, 75/75 total passing; 0 analyzer issues). Built directly on Story 2.1's auth/clinic infrastructure.

Added AuthService.signIn/signOut (Supabase signInWithPassword/signOut) and ClinicRepository.resolveRole(authUserId) (queries Users -> UserRoles -> Roles locally, maps the stored role name string back to the Role enum via Role.values.asNameMap()).

New reactive session layer: currentSessionProvider (Stream<Session?> wrapping Supabase's onAuthStateChange, which emits an initial event with the current persisted session on subscription - this is what makes "reopen app, reuse session" work without extra plumbing) -> resolvedRoleProvider (Future<Role?>, resolves the local role for the active session's user, or null if signed out).

Router redirect extended (renamed _HasLocalClinicRefreshNotifier -> _AuthRefreshNotifier, now listens to both hasLocalClinicProvider and resolvedRoleProvider): no clinic -> /sign-up; clinic exists but no resolved role (not signed in) -> /sign-in; both satisfied -> shell. Order matters: sign-up check happens before sign-in check.

New /sign-in route + SignInPage + SignInController. On successful sign-in, resolves role from local DB and calls currentRoleProvider.setRole(...), then navigates to Calendar explicitly (mirrors Story 2.1's sign-up flow). If sign-in succeeds at the Supabase layer but no local User/Role match is found for that authUserId, this is treated as an error (not silently defaulted to some role) - documented as an expected limitation until Epic 11 (sync) exists, since Users/Roles are currently local-only per device: signing in on a different device than where the account was created/added will correctly fail with "No local role found... sync is not yet implemented."

Sign-out wired into the Settings page placeholder (calls authService.signOut() + clears currentRoleProvider directly for immediate UI feedback; the reactive session stream also independently drives the router back to /sign-in).

Offline nuance (matches the PRD's own flagged assumption/open question, FR-2): reopening the app with an already-cached session works fully offline (currentSession is a local check, no network). A *fresh* sign-in attempt still requires network to call Supabase - this is inherent to any real auth backend and matches the PRD's own [ASSUMPTION]/[NEEDS VALIDATION] framing, not something this implementation needs to solve differently.
close_reason: Sign-in + persistent session implemented (Supabase auth state stream + reactive router gating), 75/75 tests passing, 0 analyzer issues

title: Story 2.1: First-Run Clinic Provisioning & Dentist Sign-Up
description: On a fresh install with no local Clinic data, sign-up creates a Clinic record (locale fr-TN, currency TND), assigns the signing-up User as Dentist with is_clinic_owner=true, and creates Role records for Dentist, Assistant, and Nurse. After sign-up user lands on Today Calendar without re-authentication. Offline during sign-up shows error; no partial data persisted. Unit test confirms all Role records are created. Part of Epic 2 (DocCentral-59s).
notes:
Implemented and tested (11 new tests, 67/67 total passing; 0 analyzer issues).

MAJOR DECISION REVERSAL: switched auth backend from Firebase (kept per an earlier Story 1.1 override) back to Supabase Auth, matching the finalized PRD (FR-1/FR-2) and architecture.md. User confirmed this explicitly after I flagged the conflict. Removed firebase_auth/firebase_core packages and lib/core/firebase/firebase_options.dart entirely; main.dart now calls Supabase.initialize() using String.fromEnvironment('SUPABASE_URL'/'SUPABASE_ANON_KEY') --dart-define values.

IMPORTANT CAVEAT: no real Supabase project is wired anywhere - the user must supply real SUPABASE_URL/SUPABASE_ANON_KEY via --dart-define when actually running the app, or sign-up will fail at the network call (expected, not a bug). Provisioning logic itself is fully unit-tested independent of a real backend via a fake AuthService.

New Drift schema (schemaVersion 2->3): Clinics extended with locale ('fr-TN' default) + currency ('TND' default); new Users/Roles/UserRoles tables (Roles uses @DataClassName('RoleRow') to avoid colliding with the existing Role RBAC enum). ClinicRepositoryImpl.provisionClinic: calls Supabase signUp first (throws AuthException, nothing touches DB on failure/offline), then atomically transacts Clinic + owner User + 3 Role rows (dentist/assistant/nurse, named via Role.name from the existing RBAC enum - no separate string-mapping table) + 1 UserRoles row.

Router: added a redirect gate in app_router.dart based on hasLocalClinicProvider (no clinic -> forced to /sign-up; clinic exists -> can't navigate back to /sign-up). Had to add a refreshListenable (_HasLocalClinicRefreshNotifier) and mark appRouterProvider @Riverpod(keepAlive:true) - without keepAlive the provider (and its internal ref.listen subscription) got disposed after the initial container.read(), breaking redirect evaluation on subsequent navigations. This is a real gotcha worth remembering for any future router-level provider-driven redirect logic.

On successful sign-up, ClinicProvisioningController also calls currentRoleProvider.notifier.setRole(Role.doctor) - this is what makes the RBAC-gated Add patient button (built in Epic 3) actually become visible in a live run for the first time.

Scope boundary respected: session persistence across restarts, sign-in screen, JWT caching are explicitly Story 2.2, not touched here.
close_reason: Sign-up + first-run clinic provisioning implemented against Supabase Auth (reversed from Firebase), 67/67 tests passing, 0 analyzer issues

title: Epic 11: Offline-First Sync
description: Delivers the complete Supabase sync engine. All workflows already function offline via Drift (established in Epic 1); this epic adds: verification that all user journeys UJ-2/3/4/10 complete offline, push of pending records to Supabase with exponential backoff retry, incremental pull of remote changes, and last-write-wins conflict resolution with explicit user warnings for Invoice and Payment conflicts. Covers FR-40, FR-41, FR-42, FR-43.

title: Epic 7: Invoicing & Payments
description: Delivers the complete financial workflow. Assistant can review the auto-generated draft invoice, add discount or surcharge adjustments as explicit line items, finalize without payment, record full or partial cash payments (multiple per invoice), and view outstanding balances per patient and clinic-wide. Dentist can void invoices. Invoice status is always derived, never manually set. Covers FR-23, FR-24, FR-25, FR-26, FR-27, FR-28, FR-49.

title: Epic 5: Clinical Visits
description: Delivers the full visit lifecycle. Assistant and Dentist can transition a checked-in visit to in-progress, add/edit/remove Performed Treatments (tooth number, procedure name, unit price, quantity), record multi-line diagnosis and clinical notes (autosave on blur), complete the visit which locks treatments and auto-generates a draft Invoice, and Dentist can unlock a completed visit if no payments exist. Covers FR-15, FR-16, FR-17, FR-18, FR-19.

title: Epic 4: Calendar & Appointments
description: Delivers the full calendar and appointment management surface. Assistant can view day-view (Today Calendar) and week-view calendars, create and edit appointments with overlap detection, cancel with required reason (including reschedule flow with linked replacement), check in patients creating a Visit, flag appointments awaiting lab work, and filter the calendar by date/patient/status/assigned user. Covers FR-9, FR-10, FR-11, FR-12, FR-13, FR-14, FR-47, FR-48.

title: Epic 3: Patient Management
description: Delivers the complete patient lifecycle. Assistant can create patients with required identity fields and backfill paper history into history_notes, search by partial name or phone (300ms on 10k records), open the full Patient File (identity, history, last 5 visits, treatment plan, outstanding balance, attachments), edit patient details with an edit log, and Dentist can soft-delete. Covers FR-4, FR-5, FR-6, FR-7, FR-8, FR-46.

title: Epic 2: Clinic Setup & Authentication
description: Delivers the complete authentication and clinic provisioning surface. Dentist signs up on a fresh install to create the clinic and initial roles, signs in with persistent session (offline-capable after first sign-in), adds Assistant or Nurse staff users from Settings, and switches the app language between FR/AR/EN with full RTL support for Arabic. Covers FR-1, FR-2, FR-3, FR-44, FR-45.

title: Story 10.3: Reopen Closed Day
description: Dentist reopens a closed day: unlocks counted_cash for re-entry, logs actor/timestamp/reason. Re-entering and confirming updates the record with new counted cash, new delta, new timestamp. Assistant returns PERMISSION_DENIED. Part of Epic 10 (DocCentral-n39).

title: Story 10.2: Record & Confirm Day Closeout
description: Assistant enters counted cash and confirms. Creates Day Closeout record: expected cash (sum of today cash Payments), counted cash (entered), delta (expected - counted), actor, UTC timestamp. One closeout per clinic per date enforced. Non-zero delta flagged visually but does not block confirmation. Unit test confirms delta is always computed, never manually set. Part of Epic 10 (DocCentral-n39).

title: Story 10.1: Day Closeout Summary
description: Day Closeout screen shows real-time summary: count of completed Visits, sum of Payments by method, sum of new Invoices, count of outstanding (unpaid/partially_paid) Invoices for current day and clinic. Updates in real time as Payments and Visits are recorded. Part of Epic 10 (DocCentral-n39).

title: Story 8.4: Low-Stock Alerts
description: Computed list of Inventory Items where on_hand <= threshold appears in dedicated Low Stock view and as count badge on Today Calendar side panel. Items appear immediately when threshold crossed. Items disappear from list immediately when on_hand rises above threshold after restock or adjustment. Tapping badge navigates to Low Stock view. Part of Epic 8 (DocCentral-p13).

title: Story 8.3: Manual Stock Adjustment
description: Assistant directly sets new on-hand quantity with a required reason to correct counting errors. Adjustment log records old quantity, new quantity, delta, reason, actor, UTC timestamp. Save rejected if reason is missing. Adjustment logs displayed separately from Restock Events (two distinct audit trails). Part of Epic 8 (DocCentral-p13).

title: Story 8.2: Record Restock Event
description: Assistant records a Restock Event on an Inventory Item: quantity added, date (default today), optional supplier, optional notes. on_hand increases by recorded amount. Event logged with actor + UTC timestamp. Restock appears in item history. Integration test confirms on_hand after restock = previous on_hand + quantity added. Part of Epic 8 (DocCentral-p13).

title: Story 8.1: Create & View Inventory Items
description: Assistant creates Inventory Items: name, category (cleaning/medicament/supply/other), unit (e.g. box of 100), initial on-hand quantity, low-stock threshold (>= 0, negative rejected). Inventory screen groups items by category showing name, unit, on-hand, threshold. Part of Epic 8 (DocCentral-p13).

title: Story 6.3: Mark Planned Treatments as Performed
description: In an in_progress Visit with attached Planned Treatments, user marks one as performed: creates Performed Treatment on the Visit (same procedure, tooth, price, quantity), transitions Planned Treatment scheduled to done. Treatment Plan progress view shows done/planned/scheduled sessions clearly. Unit test confirms marking performed creates exactly one Performed Treatment matching source. Part of Epic 6 (DocCentral-opm).

title: Story 6.2: Link Planned Treatment to Appointment
description: When creating or editing a scheduled appointment for a patient with a Treatment Plan, Assistant attaches one or more Planned Treatments. Status transitions planned to scheduled. Calendar day-view shows planned-treatment summary from linked treatments. Prevents double-booking the same Planned Treatment on two appointments. Part of Epic 6 (DocCentral-opm).

title: Story 6.1: Create Treatment Plan
description: Dentist adds Planned Treatments to a patient: procedure name (free text), tooth number, estimated unit price, sequence number, target date or next available. Status starts as planned. Treatment Plan section on Patient File lists items in sequence order with status (planned, scheduled, in_progress, done, cancelled), procedure, tooth number, target date. Unit test confirms Planned Treatment defaults to planned status. Part of Epic 6 (DocCentral-opm).

title: Story 5.5: Unlock Completed Visit
description: Dentist unlocks a completed Visit if linked Invoice is in draft with no Payments: Visit returns to in_progress, Performed Treatments editable, action logged with actor/timestamp/reason. Rejected if Invoice has any Payment (must void first) or if Invoice has been Finalized. Assistant returns PERMISSION_DENIED on any path. Part of Epic 5 (DocCentral-5mr).

title: Story 4.6: Lab Work Flag & Appointment Filters
description: Assistant sets Lab Work Flag on appointment with free-text note; flag appears in calendar views and morning side panel. Calendar filters: date range, patient name, status, assigned user. Filters compose (intersection). Single clear-all action removes all filters. Part of Epic 4 (DocCentral-rkm).

title: Story 3.5: Soft-Delete Patient
description: Dentist soft-deletes a Patient: sets deleted_at, patient disappears from search and lists. Invoices and Payments for soft-deleted patients remain visible in financial views. Assistant cannot see delete control; any path returns PERMISSION_DENIED. Part of Epic 3 (DocCentral-smy).

title: Epic 10: Day Closeout
description: Delivers end-of-day cash reconciliation. Assistant sees a real-time summary of completed visits, payments by method, and outstanding invoices, enters counted cash to create a Day Closeout record (expected vs counted, delta, actor, timestamp), one per clinic per date. Dentist can reopen a closed day to correct a missed payment. Covers FR-35, FR-36, FR-37.

title: Epic 8: Inventory Management
description: Delivers consumable inventory tracking. Assistant can create inventory items (name, category, unit, on-hand quantity, low-stock threshold), record restock events (increases on-hand, logged with actor and timestamp), make manual stock adjustments with a required reason, and see low-stock alerts in a dedicated view and as a count badge on Today Calendar side panel. Covers FR-29, FR-30, FR-31, FR-32.

title: Epic 6: Treatment Planning
description: Delivers multi-session treatment planning. Dentist can add Planned Treatments to a patient (procedure, tooth number, estimated price, sequence, target date), Assistant can attach planned treatments to appointments (transitions to scheduled status), and during an in-progress visit mark planned treatments as performed (creates Performed Treatment, transitions to done). Subject to PRD Open Q1 validation with clinic. Covers FR-20, FR-21, FR-22.

title: Re-add custom_lint and riverpod_lint when ecosystem supports riverpod_annotation 4.x
description: custom_lint and riverpod_lint were removed in Story 1.1 because they conflict with the flutter_riverpod 3.3.1 + riverpod_annotation 4.0.2 + riverpod_generator combination. Re-add both packages once upstream releases support the newer riverpod_annotation 4.x. Track: https://github.com/rrousselGit/riverpod/issues for compatibility updates.

title: Story 9.2: View & Preview Attachment
description: From Patient File or Visit view: image attachments (JPG/PNG) render inline in the DocCentral UI. PDF attachments open in the system default PDF viewer. Attachment list shows filename, file type, upload date, and uploader for each item. Part of Epic 9 (DocCentral-277).

title: Story 9.1: Upload Attachment to Patient or Visit
description: Assistant uploads a file (PDF, JPG, PNG, max 10 MB) to a Patient or Visit. File stored locally, Attachment record created linked to Patient or Visit. Files > 10 MB rejected before write. Unsupported file types rejected. Offline upload: Attachment created with sync_status=pending, queued for Supabase storage sync on connectivity. Part of Epic 9 (DocCentral-277).

title: Epic 9: File Attachments
description: Delivers file attachment management on patients and visits. Assistant can upload PDF, JPG, or PNG files (max 10 MB) to a Patient or Visit, stored locally and queued for Supabase storage sync. Files are viewable inline (images) or via system PDF viewer from the Patient File or Visit view. Covers FR-33, FR-34.
